title: Agent Aid Manifest
description: Manifest for a checked-in agent aid such as a script, runbook, prompt, template, check, or skill.

kind.description: Discriminator for agent aid manifest files.

id.description: Stable aid identifier used for inventory and review.

type.description: Kind of reusable aid described by this manifest.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the aid from candidate through shared, promoted, or retired.

scope.description: Where the aid is intended to live and who may rely on it.

portability.description: Runtime or platform portability expectation for the aid.

portability_justification.description: Why the declared portability level is acceptable.

checked_in_scope_justification.description: Why this aid belongs in checked-in repo state instead of local scratch space.

proof_role.description: Whether the aid is canonical proof, an advisory helper, or only a candidate aid.

owner.description: Owner responsible for keeping the aid useful and safe.

created_because.description: Observed friction or repeated need that justified creating the aid.

items.description: One entry in the use when list.

use_when.description: Situations where agents should consider using this aid.

entrypoint.description: Command, file, or instruction that starts use of the aid.

read_only.description: Whether this operation or aid avoids writes.

writes_repo.description: Whether this aid may modify repository files.

destructive.description: Whether this action can delete, overwrite, or otherwise destroy state.

hidden_required_workflow.description: Whether the aid secretly imposes workflow requirements; this must stay false for checked-in aids.

requires_review.description: Whether a human or stronger review pass is required before relying on the result.

safety.description: Safety properties that determine whether the aid can run automatically.

items.description: One entry in the commands list.

commands.description: Commands associated with this check, proof, or workflow step.

absent_reason.description: Reason validation commands are intentionally absent.

validation.description: How this aid is validated, or why validation is absent.

target_kind.description: Kind of destination this entry may promote or point to.

target.description: Destination file, surface, package, or record referenced by this entry.

discovery_route.description: How agents or tooling should discover the promoted artifact.

trigger.description: Condition that activates this rule, promotion, or retirement path.

retention_after_promotion.description: What happens to the original aid after promotion.

promotion.description: Promotion path if the aid should become more canonical or package-owned.

trigger.description: Condition that activates this rule, promotion, or retirement path.

retention_after_retirement.description: What happens to the aid record after retirement.

retirement.description: Conditions and retention behavior when the aid is no longer useful.
